01_initial_request.input_data: Existing literature and experimental data on GPCR structures, signaling pathways, allosteric modulators, and regulatory proteins
01_initial_request.user_intent: To review and elucidate the principles, mechanisms, and topology of allosteric regulation in GPCRs, including the role of endogenous and synthetic regulators
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Comprehensive characterization and classification of allosteric sites, regulatory mechanisms, and modulator types across specific GPCR families

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GPCR allosteric sites were known to exist but their number and localization were difficult to estimate. [Gap] Reliable identification methods for allosteric sites within the transmembrane domain were needed. [Decision] To achieve accurate site prediction, chose to use FTMap to perform computational search on GPCR structures, expecting to obtain predictive ability metrics for allosteric sites.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Predictive ability for allosteric sites located within the TMD was 80%.

references.relevance: Describes the testing of FTMap for allosteric site search in 17 GPCRs.
Early application of FTMap for GPCR allosteric space mapping.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FTMap had been tested for allosteric site search. [Gap] Improved predictive ability for allosteric sites was desired. [Decision] To achieve higher accuracy, chose to use FTSite (modified version of FTMap) to perform computational search on GPCR structures, expecting to obtain improved predictive ability metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Predictive ability for allosteric sites located within the TMD was 88%.

references.relevance: Compares FTSite predictive ability (88%) against FTMap (80%).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Allosteric sites within the TMD were being mapped. [Gap] Sites located on the outer-lateral surface interacting with membrane lipids were not well identified by FTMap/FTSite. [Decision] To identify membrane-interacting sites, chose to use Molecular Dynamics methods to perform simulation on GPCR structures, expecting to obtain identification of outer-lateral allosteric sites.

parameters.target_receptors: beta2-AR, glucagon receptor, M2-muscarinic receptor, P2Y1 purinergic receptor, D2-dopamine receptor, PAR2, C5aR1
parameters.site_location: outer-lateral surface of the TM7 bundle

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identification of allosteric sites located on the outer-lateral surface available for interaction with membrane lipids.

references.relevance: Describes dynamical correlations revealing allosteric sites.
Describes intermolecular interactions at the membrane interface from MD simulations.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Specific allosteric sites were being identified in individual receptors. [Gap] A comprehensive topology map across GPCR classes was missing. [Decision] To construct a pocketome of allosteric sites, chose to use In-silico docking of small molecules probes to perform analysis on GPCR structures, expecting to obtain a classification of allosteric sites across classes.

parameters.classes: A, B1, B2, C, D1, and F
parameters.sites_identified_class_A: up to 11 allosteric sites... and up to 8 sites that can be considered as potential

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Construction of a 'pocketome' of allosteric sites for different classes of GPCRs.

references.relevance: Describes the comprehensive analysis of topology using in-silico docking.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Computational predictions of allosteric sites required experimental validation. [Gap] Functional importance of specific residues in allosteric sites needed confirmation. [Decision] To validate the sodium ion-binding site, chose to use Site-directed mutagenesis to perform residue replacement on GPCR sequences, expecting to obtain evidence of NAM activity blockade.

parameters.effect: blocks the NAM activity of these ions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Replacement of aspartic acid with alanine blocked the NAM activity of sodium ions.

references.relevance: Describes mechanistic insights into allosteric regulation by physiological cations.
Describes sodium ion binding pocket mutations and receptor function.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Allosteric sites in TSHR were proposed based on LHR similarity. [Gap] Specific binding patterns of biased agonists needed structural explanation. [Decision] To analyze binding of biased agonists, chose to use Molecular docking to perform interaction analysis on TSHR allosteric site, expecting to obtain residue interaction patterns.

parameters.binding_site: formed by TM1, TM2, TM3, and TM7
parameters.coverage: covered from above by segments of the ECL1 and ECL2

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MSq1 binds to a site formed by TM1, TM2, TM3, and TM7, revealing differences compared to MS438.

references.relevance: Describes the Gq biased small molecule active at the TSH Receptor and docking analysis.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Synthetic allosteric regulators for beta-AR were needed. [Gap] Specific intracellular allosteric antagonists were not yet identified. [Decision] To discover new modulators, chose to use DNA-encoded small-molecule library to perform screening for beta2-AR ligands, expecting to obtain compound candidates with NAM activity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Development of Cmpd-15 with NAM activity for beta2-AR.

references.relevance: Describes isolation of small-molecule positive allosteric modulators (and Cmpd-15 NAM) from DNA-encoded libraries.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Allosteric modulators were identified computationally or via screening. [Gap] Functional activity and efficacy of modulators needed biological confirmation. [Decision] To validate modulator activity, chose to use Functional Assays (AC activity/cAMP) to perform testing on cells expressing GPCRs, expecting to obtain quantitative activity metrics.

parameters.readouts: AC activity, intracellular cAMP level, testosterone levels

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Modulators stimulated AC activity, increased cAMP levels, or inhibited agonist effects (e.g., Ladarixin IC50 0.1 nM).

references.relevance: Describes small-molecule agonists stimulating thyroid function and AC activity.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comprehensive literature review and synthesis of computational and experimental data from 447 references

allosteric_sites_identified.value: up to 11 allosteric sites... and up to 8 sites that can be considered as potential
allosteric_sites_identified.interpretation: Indicates multiplicity of allosteric sites in GPCRs.

predictive_ability_FTMap.interpretation: Overall predictive ability for all allosteric sites.

predictive_ability_FTSite.interpretation: Overall predictive ability for all allosteric sites.

receptor_families_covered.interpretation: Chemokine, PAR, TSHR, LHR, and beta-AR described in detail.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The review successfully elucidates the principles, mechanisms, and topology of allosteric regulation in GPCRs, achieving the objective of comprehensive characterization and classification across specific GPCR families.
